message:
perf(mvcc,core): adaptive chain compaction, collation registry snapshot, batch-update fence refinement, session 73 conformance tests

Four performance and correctness improvements plus new conformance
coverage.

Adaptive version chain compaction (lifecycle.rs):

- Proactive compaction threshold now adapts based on observed chain
  lengths via EWMA (exponentially weighted moving average) with
  α=0.125. Threshold = max(PROACTIVE_COMPACT_THRESHOLD, 2×EWMA),
  clamped to [lower_bound, max_chain_length/2]. This auto-tunes
  compaction aggressiveness: short chains → less GC overhead, growing
  chains → earlier intervention. Fixed-point ×256 encoding avoids
  float atomics.

- record_chain_length_sample() feeds the EWMA on every chain
  observation. adaptive_compact_threshold() replaces the static
  PROACTIVE_COMPACT_THRESHOLD in the commit-time compaction decision.

Collation registry snapshot (connection.rs):

- GROUP BY and ORDER BY sorting now snapshot the collation registry
  once before the O(N log N) sort via coll_snap = registry.clone(),
  then use cmp_sqlite_values_collated_snapshot() against the snapshot.
  Previously each comparison locked the Mutex, creating O(N log N)
  lock acquisitions for large result sets.

- PRAGMA collation_list uses lock_unpoisoned() helper for consistent
  poison handling.

- Hot-path profile snapshot now includes cached_read_snapshot_reuses
  and cached_read_snapshot_parks counters with proper reset/snapshot
  integration.

Batch commit-index fence refinement (core_types.rs):

- batch_update() early-returns for empty page lists. The upfront
  Release fence + per-page Relaxed stores pattern now has clearer
  documentation explaining the happens-before guarantee: any reader
  doing an Acquire load that observes a new commit-index value also
  sees all prior writes that preceded the Release fence.

- holder() now uses the fast-path flat atomic array for pages
  1..=65536, matching try_acquire/release.

Lock release via iterator (begin_concurrent.rs):

- release_tracked_page_locks() and held_lock_pages_iter() pass an
  iterator to release_set() instead of materializing a Vec. Updated
  across all concurrent_commit and prepare_concurrent_commit_with_ssi
  call sites. write_set_pages() now returns a borrowed slice.

Conformance tests (s55, ext):

- Session 73 batch 2 (s73k-s73q): window frame variants (ROWS
  BETWEEN, RANGE), multi-table join aggregates, RETURNING clause,
  nested correlated subqueries, UPSERT with complex ON CONFLICT,
  and CASE-driven conditional aggregates.

- Un-ignored a previously-ignored conformance test in ext.rs.

Also updates beads metadata and adds collation.rs NOCASE refinement.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
